competitive_position.summary: Zycus leads on relationship and a co-built conservative business case, but a live multi-vendor selection is sequenced after funding. Strongest threat is the unnamed field Jacquelyn flagged 18 Jun; practical incumbent is Amy's manual/unmanaged process on Oracle. No rival named, priced, or timed.

competitors.name: Unnamed multi-vendor field
competitors.quote: we've got multiple vendors that we're looking at... once we have funding it's about which vendor is right for Amy's
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor on the co-built conservative business case; name the field via Jacquelyn now; pre-stage a formal proposal, peer references, and Oracle integration design to deploy within ~10 days of funding approval.

competitors.name: Status quo (manual/unmanaged process on Oracle ERP)
competitors.quote: someone might leave you a Post-it... someone might send you an email
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of manual MRO/AP intake; lean on the restructure that 'further cements the need for P2P.'

champion_strength.summary: Two active champions co-built the business case and will personally present to the EB; advocacy is untested at executive level and Zycus is not in the funding room — so enablement is the lever.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: EB is named but not met by Zycus. Jacquelyn, 18 Jun: 'Our chief supply chain officer, Oksana, will be her in July... we're gonna do a prerun with our VP of procurement.' Oksana controls funding. When Zycus offered to attend, Jacquelyn declined: 'she is most receptive to her internal team' — so the funding pitch is champion-carried by design. The path to power here is NOT a cold Zycus approach to Oksana; it is making the champions unbeatable in a room we cannot enter. A VP-to-EB offer, if made at all, should be routed through and offered by the champion, not pushed past the declined attendance.

recommended_moves: Arm Jacquelyn and Rafal for the mid-July funding pitch: deliver the per-module cost/return breakdown, the Oracle PO/receiving integration cost assumption, and a 2-slide AI-roadmap-fit insert — every question they raised on 18 Jun, answered, in a deck they can defend without us in the room.

Offer a VP-to-EB alignment touch with Oksana THROUGH Jacquelyn (let her decide if it helps; do not push past her stated preference). If declined, ensure the champion deck carries a peer MRO/P2P reference in her place.
Name the competitive field: ask Jacquelyn directly for the other vendors and the selection criteria so we can pre-empt them in the post-funding bake-off.
